steps.title: Challenge communication as purpose
steps.content: The second team's claim depends on the sounds being useful for communication with other caterpillars. Evidence that the sound-producing caterpillars are usually solitary would directly weaken that proposed purpose.

steps.title: Focus on solitary caterpillars
steps.content: Choice A addresses whether predators can hear the sounds, not whether caterpillars communicate with each other. Choice B concerns species that did not make sounds during attacks, and Choice C only shows when the sounds occurred. The remaining finding removes likely recipients for communication. The answer is D.
